commit.message:
FLTKContextManager: create real shared GLX inner contexts to fix scene_texture crash (#380)

* Initial plan

* FLTKContextManager: create real shared GLX inner contexts for proper GL isolation

Fixes _mesa_ReadPixels crash in SoSceneTexture2P::updatePBuffer when the
Rendering/scene_texture demo is shown in the obol_viewer System GL panel.

Root cause: SoOffscreenRenderer sets SORTED_OBJECT_BLEND transparency which
forces updatePBuffer (not updateFrameBuffer). updatePBuffer needs real GL
context isolation — it assumes makeContextCurrent switches to a fresh inner
context so that glReadPixels sees only tmpFbo, not the outer renderer's FBO.
The old FLTKContextManager shared one GL context for everything, so the outer
FBO binding leaked into the inner "context", causing the Mesa crash.

Fix (X11/GLX): ensureWindow() now records mainGLCtx_ and mainDrawable_ after
the first make_current(). createOffscreenContext() creates a real GLXContext
via glXCreateContext(dpy, vi, mainGLCtx_, True) — sharing textures/VBOs with
the FLTK context but having isolated GL state. makeContextCurrent() uses
glXMakeCurrent() to actually switch; restorePreviousContext() switches back.
Windows/macOS fall back to the original single-context approach (unchanged).

Co-authored-by: starseeker <238416+starseeker@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(dns_record): add input validation, error handling, and zone ID caching

- Assert required variables upfront for clear failure messages
- Add failed_when checking Cloudflare API success field to catch
  logical failures returned with HTTP 200
- Add changed_when for accurate idempotency reporting
- Use verbosity-gated no_log so errors are visible with -vvv
- Cache zone ID across multiple role inclusions in same play
- Add meta/main.yml matching project conventions

* docs: update DNS documentation for automatic provisioning

DNS A records are now auto-provisioned during app deployment via the
dns_record role. Update first-deployment guide, managing-records,
batch-operations, cloudflare-setup, infrastructure-layers, ansible-roles
guide, and troubleshooting docs to reflect this change.

* feat(cockpit): add dns_record integration with Tailscale IP

Cockpit binds to the Tailscale interface, so its DNS A record must
point to the Tailscale IP rather than the public VPS IP.

* fix(dns_record): validate Cloudflare success flag on all API calls

- Add failed_when on zone lookup and record lookup GET requests
- Check for exactly 1 zone result instead of just non-empty
- Use verbosity-gated no_log on assert task

* fix(dns_record): domain-scoped cache, IPv4 validation, drift detection

- Cache zone IDs in a dict keyed by domain (safe for multi-domain plays)
- Validate dns_record_ip matches IPv4 format in assert block
- Fail explicitly when multiple A records found for same FQDN
- Update task now triggers on IP, TTL, or proxied changes (not just IP)

* docs: add cockpit to integrated apps lists, fix troubleshooting causes

- Broaden batch-operations example to include all non-integrated apps
- Rewrite troubleshooting causes to reflect realistic failure scenarios

- Update managing-records example to show only non-integrated apps

  in CI logs or shared terminals at any verbosity level
- Surface Cloudflare API errors via separate fail tasks that log only
  sanitized json.errors field

* fix: add enriched virtual datasets for Data Detail charts in Learner Engagement dashboard

Create three virtual Superset datasets that join raw dimensional fact
tables with dimension/intermediate tables to provide the columns needed
by the Data Detail Navigation, Video, and Problems charts:

- tfact_course_navigation_events_enriched: adds course_title via join
  to int__combined__course_runs
- tfact_video_events_enriched: adds video_name (dim_video) and
  course_title (int__combined__course_runs)
- tfact_problem_events_enriched: adds problem_name, markdown,
  max_attempts, weight (dim_problem) and course_title
  (int__combined__course_runs)

Update the three chart YAMLs to reference the new virtual dataset UUIDs
instead of the raw physical datasets that lacked these columns.

These virtual datasets are a temporary fix; follow-up work will create
proper dbt reporting models so they can be replaced with physical datasets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use existing Data_Detail virtual datasets for Learner Engagement Data Detail charts

Rather than creating new virtual datasets, redirect the three Data Detail
charts to the pre-existing virtual datasets that already have the
necessary SQL joins:

- Data Detail Navigation → Data_Detail_Nav (32e283f5)
  joins tfact_course_navigation_events with int__combined__course_runs
  for course_title

- Data Detail Video → Data_Detail_Video (3967f60c)
  joins tfact_video_events with dim_video for video_name and
  int__combined__course_runs for course_title

- Data Detail Problems → Data_Detail_Problems (dc0886e8)
  joins tfact_problem_events with dim_problem for problem_name,
  markdown, max_attempts, weight and int__combined__course_runs
  for course_title

Also removes the redundant _enriched_ virtual datasets added in the
previous commit, as they duplicate the existing Data_Detail_* datasets.

Virtual datasets are exempt from chart-column validation (via the
validator's virtual dataset handling), so pointing charts at them
eliminates the false-positive column-missing warnings cleanly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: sync Superset asset metadata from auto-refresh

Commit automated metadata updates pulled by sup from the live Superset
instance (timestamps, column type changes, reformatting). The two dataset
redirects that fix the Learner Engagement dashboard charts are preserved:
- afact_problem_engagement (37bb25c7) → engagement_problem_completion_raw
- afact_course_page_engagement (68c544d7) → page_engagement_views_report

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ol-superset): delete progress.log before each sync to ensure all assets are pushed

The sup CLI writes a progress.log file (relative to cwd) that records
which assets have already been pushed. On subsequent sync runs it skips
assets already present in that log, silently reporting success while
leaving stale versions in the target instance.

Delete progress.log at the start of each sync (after UUID mapping, before
the first sup push) so every run pushes all assets fresh.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ol-superset): delete progress.log before promote push steps

Same fix as sync: remove sup's progress.log after UUID mapping so that
all assets are pushed to production fresh on every promotion run, rather
than being silently skipped if they appeared in a previous run's log.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ol-superset): patch physical dataset table connections after import

Superset's import API (/api/v1/assets/import/) does not update table_name
or schema for existing physical datasets — it only updates metadata (columns,
metrics, etc.). Add sync_physical_dataset_connections() which, after each
dataset push, fetches each physical dataset from the target instance by UUID
and explicitly PATCHes table_name and schema via PUT /api/v1/dataset/{id}
if they differ from the local YAML.

Wired into both sync and promote commands as a post-push step.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ol-superset): revert afact_course_page_engagement dataset to original table

No chart uses this dataset UUID (68c544d7) anymore — the Page Engagement By
Section chart now points directly at page_engagement_views_report (d7152969).
Keeping the redirect caused a 422 Unprocessable Entity conflict when syncing
because Superset enforces uniqueness on (database, catalog, schema, table_name)
and page_engagement_views_report_d7152969 already claims that table.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Previously SQL/CUSTOM expression types in chart params were either
skipped or only inspected for their structured SIMPLE fields.

commit.message:
Fix Slice shape inference crashes with SIGABRT on empty dimensions (#7738)

Fix #7735 

This pull request addresses issues with the ONNX Slice operator when
handling empty dimensions, ensuring correct and safe shape inference in
these edge cases. The main focus is to prevent undefined behavior and
ensure that slicing on empty dimensions consistently produces outputs
with zero length, regardless of the step direction. Additional
regression tests are included to verify these fixes, and several test
data files are updated to reflect the new behavior.

**Slice operator improvements for empty dimensions:**

* Updated the `processSliceInputs` function in `defs.cc` to
short-circuit and produce a zero-length output when the input dimension
size is zero, avoiding invalid clamp bounds and potential undefined
behavior.
* Modified shape inference logic in `old.cc` to detect empty dimensions

commit.message:
feat(ol-superset): comprehensive chart column reference extraction

Previously only groupby (plain strings), all_columns (plain strings),
x_axis, and SIMPLE adhoc_filters were checked. Charts also reference
dataset columns through:

- metrics / metric / secondary_metric / timeseries_limit_metric /
  percent_metrics: SIMPLE-type metric dicts carry the aggregated column
  in column.column_name; SQL-expression metrics are skipped
- groupby / all_columns adhoc-column dicts: SIMPLE type exposes the
  underlying column via column.column_name; SQL type is skipped
- entity: plain string column used in map/scatter charts
- order_by_cols: JSON-encoded ["column_name", bool] strings

Added helpers:
  _col_name_from_adhoc() — extracts column from SIMPLE adhoc-column dict
  _col_name_from_metric() — extracts column from SIMPLE metric dict
  _cols_from_order_by_cols() — parses JSON sort entries

14 new unit tests (81 total); all pass. The validate command now
surfaces previously hidden mismatches from metrics and sort columns.

feat(ol-superset): add impact and lineage commands

- impact: git-change blast-radius report + orphan detection
  - Classifies changed files as dbt models, datasets, charts, or dashboards
  - Builds a reverse dependency graph and traces transitively affected assets
  - --base <ref>: compare HEAD to any git ref (e.g. origin/main)
  - --staged: only staged changes vs HEAD (default: working tree vs HEAD)
  - --orphans: report charts not in any dashboard + datasets not in any chart
  - --format json: machine-readable output for CI/pipeline integration
  - dbt _*.yml schema files are parsed to extract the model names they define

- lineage: full Dashboard → Chart → Dataset → dbt model dependency graph
  - --format text|json|mermaid output
  - --dashboard filter by title substring
  - --dbt-dir to resolve physical datasets to their dbt model + layer

commit.message:
perf: 6 targeted performance improvements

P1-A event_database.py: threading.local() persistent SQLite connection
  - Replace new sqlite3.connect() per call with reusable per-thread conn
  - Eliminates connection overhead on every save_event/get_events call
  - Expected: 10-50x speedup on monitoring writes

P1-B skill_manager.py: O(1) built-in prompt index
  - Add _builtin_prompt_index dict built once at _ensure_init()
  - Replace two O(n) BUILTIN_SKILLS linear scans with O(1) dict lookup

P1-C agent_routes.py: in-memory LRU chat history cache
  - Module-level _history_cache (OrderedDict, max 50 sessions)
  - _load_history() checks cache before disk read (cache hit = zero I/O)
  - _save_history() updates cache + disk in sync
  - Expected: near-zero disk reads for multi-turn conversations

P2-A ai_router.py: thread-safe cache + timeout/fallback improvements
  - Add _cache_lock (threading.Lock) to all _cache mutations
  - Expand cache 100 -> 256 entries
  - Reduce default timeout 3.0s -> 2.0s
  - Return ('CHAT','Timeout-fallback','AI') on timeout instead of None
  - Update tests to match new cache size

P2-B smart_dispatcher.py: LRU result cache (128 entries)
  - Add _route_cache (OrderedDict, max 128) keyed by MD5(user_input)
  - Cache full analyze() result for repeated/similar queries
  - Skip cache when file_context is present (state-dependent)

P2-C web/settings.py: write-coalescing with dirty flag
  - set()/update() mark _dirty + schedule 2s delayed flush via Timer
  - flush() writes to disk only when dirty, called at atexit
  - Bulk settings changes now result in 1 disk write instead of N

All 467 tests pass.

commit.message:
feat(ol-superset): sqlglot column validation for virtual datasets + SELECT * warnings

Uses sqlglot to parse virtual dataset SQL queries and validate that every
column referenced by a dependent chart actually appears in the query output.
Also warns when virtual datasets use SELECT * or table.* wildcards that
prevent static column analysis.

Changes:

asset_index.py:
- Add sqlglot + sqlglot.expressions imports
- Add VirtualColumnsResult(NamedTuple) with columns: set[str]|None and
  has_wildcard: bool fields
- Add extract_virtual_dataset_columns(sql) — parses outermost SELECT,
  returns exact lowercase column names; returns (None, has_wildcard=True)
  for SELECT */table.* and (None, has_wildcard=False) on parse failure
- DatasetAsset gains virtual_columns: set[str]|None and sql_has_wildcard: bool
- _load_datasets() calls extract_virtual_dataset_columns() for every virtual
  dataset and stores the result on the DatasetAsset

validate.py (section [4/4]):
- 4b: warn when dataset.sql_has_wildcard — surface it as an actionable
  message with the suggestion to replace with an explicit column list
- 4c: for virtual datasets, compare chart column refs against
  virtual_columns (case-insensitive lowercase); skip only when
  virtual_columns is None (wildcard or unparseable)
- 4c: for simple datasets, continue using columns|calculated_columns as before
- Distinguish message wording: 'SQL output' vs 'column list' per type

pyproject.toml:
- Add sqlglot>=20.0 to runtime dependencies

tests/test_asset_index.py (+9 tests, 37 total):
- Updated fixture: virtual dataset uses explicit SELECT columns; added
  separate wildcard dataset (uuid=pqr999, SELECT a.*)
- test_build_asset_index_virtual_dataset: assert virtual_columns parsed
- test_build_asset_index_wildcard_dataset: assert sql_has_wildcard=True
- 8 new extract_virtual_dataset_columns tests covering: simple SELECT,
  aliases, case normalisation, CTE outer SELECT, bare SELECT *, table.*,
  parse error, empty string

Results on real assets: 2 SELECT * warnings for Program_Enrollment_with_user
and marts__mitxonline_course_engagements_daily; section_title genuinely
missing from marts__combined_video_engagements_w_video_counts SQL output
now correctly surfaced.
